untranslatable_reason: The Double Bottom pattern requires multi-bar pattern recognition (identifying two distinct bottoms, measuring the peak between them as confirmation point, and distinguishing Adam vs Eve variants). This requires historical bar-by-bar shape analysis and relative extrema detection that cannot be expressed using only single-bar indicators and the allowed operators (crosses_above, crosses_below, comparison operators). A proper implementation would need a custom pattern detector or multi-bar lookback logic not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: Signal describes a classic chart pattern that requires visual/structural pattern recognition. The core logic (price rises above confirmation point after two bottoms) is approximated using pivot crossover as a proxy for breakout detection, but this does not capture the essential double-bottom morphology. Expected success rate ~33% per source. Manual pattern detection or custom indicator required for accurate implementation.
